disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Progressive chest tightness and difficulty exhaling over 6 hours, following a recent cold
trigger_question_examples: What's been happening with your breathing?
When did this start?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's been building up for about six hours since I came down with a cold — my chest is tight and I can't get the air back out.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reliever inhaler used about 6 times in 3 hours with diminishing effect and is now empty
trigger_question_examples: Have you used your reliever, and is it helping?
How many times have you taken the blue puffer and did it work?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've used the blue puffer about six times in the last three hours and it's helping less and less — now it's empty and it isn't working at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Able to speak only in short phrases, not full sentences, because of breathlessness
trigger_question_examples: Can you speak a full sentence in one breath?
Are you able to finish your sentences?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — I can only get a few words out before I have to stop and breathe.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Visible use of neck and chest muscles to breathe, with fast breathing and tripod posture
trigger_question_examples: Can you see him using his neck or chest muscles to breathe?
Is he breathing fast or sitting in a particular way?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's breathing really fast and you can see his neck and chest muscles pulling with every breath — he's leaning forward on his hands.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Home fingertip oximeter reads 93 percent oxygen saturation
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a pulse oximeter or smartwatch that shows an oxygen level?
What does the oximeter read?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The little finger clip says 93 percent.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms triggered by a recent viral upper-respiratory infection (a cold)
trigger_question_examples: Have you had a cold or any infection recently?
Do you know what set this off?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've had a bit of a cold the last couple of days and this started off the back of it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Daily preventer inhaler has been taken only irregularly over the last month
trigger_question_examples: How have you been going with the daily preventer puffer?
Have you been able to keep up with the preventer every day?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly, I haven't been great with the preventer — I keep forgetting it and haven't used it much this past month.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: It's fine, I don't really need the preventer, the blue one usually sorts me out.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No previous intensive-care admission or ventilation for asthma, but never this severe before
trigger_question_examples: Have you ever been admitted to intensive care or put on a ventilator for your asthma?
Has an attack ever been this bad before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've never been in intensive care for it, but it's honestly never felt this bad.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient is highly anxious and frightened about not being able to get air in
trigger_question_examples: How are you feeling in yourself right now?
Are you feeling frightened or panicky with this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm really scared — it feels like I can't get enough air and that's terrifying.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Currently no blue lips and the patient is fully awake and alert
trigger_question_examples: Are his lips or face going blue at all?
Is he staying fully awake, or getting drowsy or confused?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blue lips yet and he's wide awake — just really struggling and frightened.
